underlying: Whether/when Kash Patel leaves as FBI Director (4 cumulative date strikes: before May 1, Jun 1, Jul 1, Aug 1)
current_value_source: Daily Beast 4/27, Townhall 4/28, multiple outlets confirm Patel still FBI Director as of 4/28; reports of impending firing but no official announcement
method: Cumulative survival-style hazard model. P(out by Aug 1) > P(out by Jul 1) > P(out by Jun 1) > P(out by May 1). News flow as of 4/27-28: (a) Politico/Daily Beast story 'matter of time'; (b) White House anonymous official quoted; (c) Patel suing The Atlantic for defamation over MIA report; (d) Trump 'fed up' per Yahoo report; (e) Patel's iPhone-fumble video viral after Apr 27 shooting. Historical base: Trump cabinet/dir ousters typically follow weeks of leaks then a sudden Truth Social post. From 'matter of time' status the empirical hazard is roughly 5-10%/week. P(out by May 1) = ~5% (only ~2.5 days remaining; one big news cycle could trigger but Trump has not yet posted). P(out by Jun 1) = ~38% (full month, momentum building, 4 weeks * ~9-10% weekly hazard with some autocorrelation). P(out by Jul 1) = ~58% (additional month, hazard slowly decays as Patel survives news cycles). P(out by Aug 1) = ~68% (6mo to election season hazard plateau). Market is priced cumulatively: 0.054 / 0.42 / 0.60 / 0.66. My estimates: 0.05 / 0.38 / 0.58 / 0.68. Very close to market — slight long bias on Aug 1 (model 0.68 vs market 0.66) and short bias on Jun 1 (model 0.38 vs 0.42).
notes: Confirmed event semantics from Kalshi API: each market is a cumulative-by-date binary (resolves YES if Patel leaves before that date). Strikes form a monotonic hazard curve. Market last prices reveal heavy speculation that something dramatic happens in May (May 1 → Jun 1 jumps from 0.05 to 0.42 = 37pp of mass implies the market expects a ~88% conditional probability of firing in May given still-in-role on May 1). That seems aggressive — anonymous White House sources drive sentiment but Trump is famously unpredictable on cabinet timing. My model has slightly thinner May tail (33pp vs market 37pp) and slightly fatter Jul-Aug (28pp combined vs market 24pp). Risk asymmetries: (1) Patel's Atlantic lawsuit may signal he's digging in to fight, lowering near-term ouster odds. (2) FBI Director firing requires no Senate process — purely Trump's call. (3) The shooting incident on Apr 27 + iPhone video is the kind of viral embarrassment that could trigger a same-week firing. (4) Newsweek prediction-market roundup cites '23% by April 30, 67% by June 30, 80% by December 31' which is HIGHER than the current Kalshi book — possibly stale. Conf=medium-low because: insider rumors are loud but unsubstantiated, Trump's behavior on this kind of firing is hard to model, and small movements in news could shift these prices 10pp in a day.
